en.extract:
The indigo flycatcher (Eumyias indigo) is a species of bird in the Old World flycatcher family, Muscicapidae.
It is found in Indonesia and Malaysia, where it is found in Sumatra, Java and northern montane areas of Borneo.
Its natural habitat is tropical moist submontane montane forests between 900m to 3000m, where it is a common to fairly common species.

The '''indigo flycatcher''' ('''''Eumyias indigo''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the Old World flycatcher family, [[Muscicapidae]].<ref>Del Hoyo, J.; Elliot, A. & Christie D. (editors). (2006). ''[[Handbook of the Birds of the World]]''. Volume 11: Old World Flycatchers to Old World Warblers. Lynx Edicions. {{ISBN|84-96553-06-X}}.</ref>
It is found in [[Indonesia]] and [[Malaysia]], where it is found in [[Sumatra]], [[Java]] and northern montane areas of [[Borneo]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist submontane [[montane forest]]s between 900m to 3000m, where it is a common to fairly common species.
